channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Brico Depôt lleva a cabo una total transformación de la compañía gracias a la digitalización
item.description: El auge cada vez mayor de la tendencia ‘do it yourself‘ (DIY) y el creciente interés de los usuarios por renovar su hogar ha propiciado un exponencial crecimiento del mercado del bricolaje en los últimos años.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI could save the public sector billions — if it is willing to open up
item.description: The public sector is yet to embrace AI, but Google says it could save up to £38bn in the next 5 years
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Kotlin to lose scripting features
item.description: JetBrains plans to drop some scripting-related technologies from the Kotlin language, including REPL functionality, an obsolete API for Java scripting, the KotlinScriptMojo Maven plugin, and the kotlin-scripting-ide-services library for implementing code completion functionality.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Federal prosecutors seek records from company that deployed AI weapons scanner on NYC subway
item.description: Federal investigators in New York have requested records from the manufacturer of an AI-powered weapons scanner that was briefly deployed in New York City’s subway system.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US scientists may have developed the first robot syllabus that allows machines to transfer skills without human intervention
item.description: RoVi-Aug framework could enhance robotic skill transfer across diverse environments and hardware configurations.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Explicit ‘deepfake' images of nearly 50 teens rocks Pennsylvania school
item.description: Students have used AI technology to create fake explicit images of classmates in Washington, California and New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Rep. Lauren Boebert Briefly Joins Cameo to Sell Personalized Videos
item.description: Rep. Lauren Boebert's brief stint on Cameo raises ethics concerns and sparks questions about House rules.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: Build generative AI pipelines without the infrastructure headache
item.description: While creating a basic ChatGPT prototype might take a weekend, developing production-ready generative AI systems that securely handle enterprise data presents significantly greater engineering challenges.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The engine of Japan's flagship new small rocket explodes during a test for a second time
item.description: The engine for a flagship new small Japanese rocket has burst into flames during a combustion test, but there was no injury or damage to the outside, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: USPTO petitioned to cancel Oracle’s JavaScript trademark
item.description: Deno Land, maker of the Deno runtime for JavaScript, has petitioned the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) to cancel Oracle’s ownership of the JavaScript trademark.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia’s new AI model Fugatto can generate music and audio
item.description: Nvidia on Monday showed a new artificial intelligence model for generating music and audio that can modify voices and generate novel sounds — technology aimed at the producers of music, films and video games.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Rethinking defense technology: A blueprint for US success in a complex global landscape
item.description: The Defense Department has made efforts to enhance its AI capabilities through the AI Adoption Strategy. The post Rethinking defense technology: A blueprint for US success in a complex global landscape first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Is Outlook down? Thousands of Microsoft 365 users report outage issues
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Thousands of Microsoft 365 customers worldwide reported having issues with services like Outlook and Teams on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The best cozy games during a stressful Thanksgiving season
item.description: For many, video games are loud, violent, and competitive. But for some they are the opposite: quiet, soft, and low-stakes. Welcome to the world of cozy gaming.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Mr. Miyagi's dramatic return
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bitcoin ticks closer to $100,000 in extended surge following US elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin extended its streak of record highs after ticking above $99,000 for the first time. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court allows multibillion-dollar class action to proceed against Meta
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court is allowing a multibillion-dollar class action investors’ lawsuit to proceed against Facebook parent Meta, stemming from the privacy scandal involving the Cambridge Analytica political consulting firm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to bring Android 16’s Notification Cooldown brilliance to any phone today
item.description: Well, I’ll be: We’ve just barely finished welcoming Google’s Android 15 update into the world, and already, Android 16 is teasing us with a tiny early taste. Yes, indeedly: Google has now officially launched the first developer preview of next year’s Android 16 software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Red Hat Linux to be official WSL distro
item.description: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) is set to become an official Linux distribution for Microsoft’s Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL), a Windows feature that allows users to run Linux environments on Windows without a separate virtual machine.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety as Trump's vow to undo Biden's AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has vowed to repeal President Joe Biden's signature artificial intelligence policy when he returns to the White House for a second term. What that actually means for the future of AI technology remains to be seen.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Azure Container Apps launches Python, JavaScript interpreters
item.description: Python code interpreter sessions are generally available for Microsoft’s Azure Container Apps serverless platform for running applications and microservices. The service is also offering custom dynamic sessions, with JavaScript code interpreter capabilities in public preview.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KRON4
